# Zero to MLE — LLM Context File

> This file helps AI assistants understand and navigate this repository effectively.

## What This Repo Is

A structured 24-week roadmap for software engineers transitioning to Machine Learning Engineer (MLE) roles at Amazon, Meta, Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic. It includes week-by-week learning plans, project templates, curated resources, and progress tracking.

## How to Navigate

- `weeks/phase-{N}-{name}/README.md` — Detailed weekly plans with resources, exercises, and deliverables
- `weeks/phase-{N}-{name}/qa-*.md` — Q&A knowledge base files, co-located with the topic they cover
- `projects/project-{N}-{name}/README.md` — Project templates with architecture, requirements, and structure
- `resources/{type}.md` — Curated lists of books, courses, papers, tools
- `tracking/{type}.md` — Progress tracking templates (LeetCode, blogs, interviews)
- `PROGRESS.md` — Master checklist for all 24 weeks
- `QA_GUIDE.md` — How the Q&A system works, format template
- `SYNC_GUIDE.md` — How forked users pull upstream updates without conflicts

## Phases

1. Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): Math Foundations — linear algebra, probability, calculus, optimization
2. Phase 2 (Weeks 5–10): Core ML — linear models, trees, ensembles, evaluation, feature engineering
3. Phase 3 (Weeks 11–16): Deep Learning — neural nets from scratch, PyTorch, CNNs, Transformers
4. Phase 4 (Weeks 17–20): LLMs & GenAI — prompting, RAG, fine-tuning (LoRA/RLHF), AI agents
5. Phase 5 (Weeks 21–23): MLOps — model serving, pipelines, CI/CD, monitoring, drift detection
6. Phase 6 (Week 24+): Interview Prep — ML system design, coding, behavioral (Amazon LPs)

## How Users Use This Repo

- **Fork** the repo to get their own copy
- Check off items in PROGRESS.md as they complete them
- Build projects in the `projects/` directories
- Push progress to their fork (builds GitHub contribution graph)

## Key Design Principles

- Every week produces a tangible output (code, blog post, or deployed artifact)
- Online visibility (blogging, GitHub, LinkedIn) is integrated into every phase
- Resources are opinionated — one primary resource per topic, no decision paralysis
- Aligned to real interview requirements at top AI companies (2025–2026 job descriptions)
- Production-focused: deploy, monitor, and maintain — not just train models
